description.pt:
O script resolve a complexidade de criar e exportar animações e passeios virtuais diretamente no SketchUp. Ele permite organizar diferentes visões de câmera em uma linha do tempo interativa e exportar vídeos mp4 em diversos formatos (como retrato, paisagem ou personalizado) através da integração com o FFmpeg, facilitando a apresentação de projetos 3D.

Principais Funcionalidades:
- Gerenciamento de Linha do Tempo (Timeline) interativa, permitindo adicionar visões de câmera, ajustar o tempo, e aplicar transições suaves (Easing) ou repetição (Loop).
- Exportação de vídeo automatizada usando o FFmpeg, com suporte a resoluções predefinidas (Paisagem 16:9, Retrato 9:16, Quadrado 1:1, etc.) e personalizadas.
- Sistema de backup e compartilhamento que permite importar e exportar projetos inteiros ou linhas do tempo individuais no formato JSON.
- Preparação inteligente de ambiente (no Windows) que oculta automaticamente janelas sobrepostas durante a gravação para garantir uma captura de tela limpa.
description.en:
This script solves the complexity of creating and exporting animations and virtual tours directly in SketchUp. It allows you to organize different camera views in an interactive timeline and export MP4 videos in various formats (such as portrait, landscape, or custom) through integration with FFmpeg, facilitating the presentation of 3D projects.

Key Features:
- Interactive Timeline Management, allowing you to add camera views, adjust timing, and apply smooth (Easing) or loop transitions.
- Automated video export using FFmpeg, supporting predefined (Landscape 16:9, Portrait 9:16, Square 1:1, etc.) and custom resolutions.
- Backup and sharing system that allows you to import and export entire projects or individual timelines in JSON format.
- Intelligent environment preparation (on Windows) that automatically hides overlapping windows during recording to ensure a clean screen capture.
description.es:
Este script simplifica la creación y exportación de animaciones y recorridos virtuales directamente en SketchUp. Permite organizar diferentes vistas de cámara en una línea de tiempo interactiva y exportar vídeos MP4 en diversos formatos (como vertical, horizontal o personalizado) mediante la integración con FFmpeg, facilitando la presentación de proyectos 3D.

Características principales:
- Gestión interactiva de la línea de tiempo, que permite añadir vistas de cámara, ajustar la sincronización y aplicar transiciones suaves (Easing) o en bucle.

- Exportación automática de vídeo mediante FFmpeg, compatible con resoluciones predefinidas (Horizontal 16:9, Vertical 9:16, Cuadrado 1:1, etc.) y personalizadas.

- Sistema de copia de seguridad y uso compartido que permite importar y exportar proyectos completos o líneas de tiempo individuales en formato JSON.

- Preparación inteligente del entorno (en Windows) que oculta automáticamente las ventanas superpuestas durante la grabación para garantizar una captura de pantalla nítida.

FFmpeg (Mantido) --- FFMPEG_INSTALL_SCRIPT ||= <<~'RUBY' require 'sketchup' require 'open-uri' require 'fileutils' require 'tmpdir' URL_WIN ||= 'https://github.com/BtbN/FFmpeg-Builds/releases/download/latest/ffmpeg-master-latest-win64-gpl.zip' URL_MAC ||= 'https://evermeet.cx/ffmpeg/getrelease/zip' win = Sketchup.platform == :platform_win major = Sketchup.version.to_s[/^\d+/].to_i sketchup_year = 2000 + major plugins_dir = Sketchup.find_support_file('Plugins') if !plugins_dir || plugins_dir.empty? || !Dir.exist?(plugins_dir) if win base = ENV['APPDATA'].to_s plugins_dir = File.join(base, 'SketchUp', "SketchUp #{sketchup_year}", 'SketchUp', 'Plugins') else plugins_dir = File.expand_path("~/Library/Application Support/SketchUp #{sketchup_year}/SketchUp/Plugins") end end target_dir = File.join(plugins_dir, 'ScriptUp', 'ffmpeg') target_exe = File.join(target_dir, win ? 'ffmpeg.exe' : 'ffmpeg') url = win ? URL_WIN : URL_MAC default_zip_name = win ? 'ffmpeg-win64-gpl-latest.zip' : 'ffmpeg-macos-release.zip' proceed = true unless plugins_dir && !plugins_dir.empty? UI.messagebox(ScriptUpComunidade::TourVirtual.obter_texto(:ffmpeg_plugins_erro)) proceed = false end if proceed msg = "#{ScriptUpComunidade::TourVirtual.obter_texto(:ffmpeg_instalar_em)}\n#{target_dir}\n\n#{ScriptUpComunidade::TourVirtual.obter_texto(:ffmpeg_origem)}\n#{url}\n\n#{ScriptUpComunidade::TourVirtual.obter_texto(:ffmpeg_continuar)}" if UI.messagebox(msg, MB_YESNO) != IDYES UI.messagebox(ScriptUpComunidade::TourVirtual.obter_texto(:ffmpeg_cancelada)) proceed = false end end if proceed && File.exist?(target_exe) overwrite = UI.messagebox("#{ScriptUpComunidade::TourVirtual.obter_texto(:ffmpeg_ja_existe)}\n#{target_exe}\n\n#{ScriptUpComunidade::TourVirtual.obter_texto(:ffmpeg_sobrescrever)}", MB_YESNO) if overwrite != IDYES UI.messagebox(ScriptUpComunidade::TourVirtual.obter_texto(:ffmpeg_cancelada)) proceed = false end end if proceed Dir.mktmpdir('ffmpeg_dl_') do |tmp| zip_path = File.join(tmp, default_zip_name) extract_dir = File.join(tmp, 'unzipped') begin URI.open(url, 'rb') do |io| File.open(zip_path, 'wb') { |f| IO.copy_stream(io, f) } end Dir.mkdir(extract_dir) if win ps = 'powershell -NoProfile -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -Command' cmd = "#{ps} \"Expand-Archive -LiteralPath '#{zip_path}' -DestinationPath '#{extract_dir}' -Force\"" ok = system(cmd) raise ScriptUpComunidade::TourVirtual.obter_texto(:ffmpeg_falha_expand) unless ok else ok = system("unzip -o -q '#{zip_path}' -d '#{extract_dir}'") raise ScriptUpComunidade::TourVirtual.obter_texto(:ffmpeg_falha_unzip) unless ok end pattern = win ? '**/ffmpeg.exe' : '**/ffmpeg' bin = Dir.glob(File.join(extract_dir, pattern)).find { |p| File.file?(p) } raise ScriptUpComunidade::TourVirtual.obter_texto(:ffmpeg_exe_nao_encontrado) unless bin FileUtils.mkdir_p(target_dir) unless Dir.exist?(target_dir) FileUtils.cp(bin, target_exe) unless win FileUtils.chmod(0o755, target_exe) system("xattr -d com.apple.quarantine '#{target_exe}' 2>/dev/null") end UI.messagebox("#{ScriptUpComunidade::TourVirtual.obter_texto(:ffmpeg_sucesso)}\n#{target_exe}") puts "OK: #{target_exe}" rescue => e UI.messagebox("#{ScriptUpComunidade::TourVirtual.obter_texto(:ffmpeg_falhou)} #{e.message}\n#{ScriptUpComunidade::TourVirtual.obter_texto(:ffmpeg_abrindo_nav)}") UI.openURL(url) end end end RUBY # --- Módulo WindowUtils --- module WindowUtils extend Fiddle::Importer dlload 'user32.dll' if Sketchup.platform == :platform_win if Sketchup.platform == :platform_win typealias 'HWND', 'void*' extern 'HWND GetActiveWindow()' extern 'HWND GetForegroundWindow()' extern 'HWND GetWindow(HWND, int)' extern 'int GetWindowRect(HWND, void*)' extern 'HWND GetParent(HWND)' extern 'int ShowWindow(HWND, int)' extern 'int SetWindowPos(HWND, HWND, int, int, int, int, unsigned int)' extern 'int IsWindowVisible(HWND)' extern 'unsigned long GetWindowThreadProcessId(HWND, void*)' extern 'HWND GetDesktopWindow()' GW_CHILD = 5 unless defined?(GW_CHILD) GW_HWNDNEXT = 2 unless defined?(GW_HWNDNEXT) SW_HIDE = 0 unless defined?(SW_HIDE) SW_SHOW = 5 unless defined?(SW_SHOW) SWP_NOMOVE = 0x0002 unless defined?(SWP_NOMOVE) SWP_NOSIZE = 0x0001 unless defined?(SWP_NOSIZE) Rect = struct ['long left', 'long top', 'long right', 'long bottom'] unless defined?(Rect) @hidden_windows = [] @topmost_hwnd = nil end def self.get_viewport_rect_deep(view) return nil unless Sketchup.platform == :platform_win hwnd = GetForegroundWindow() return nil if hwnd.nil? || hwnd == 0 vw = view.vpwidth vh = view.vpheight found = find_recursive(hwnd, vw, vh) return found if found parent = GetParent(hwnd) if parent && parent != 0 found = find_recursive(parent, vw, vh) return found if found end nil end def self.find_recursive(parent_hwnd, target_w, target_h) child = GetWindow(parent_hwnd, GW_CHILD) while child && child.to_i != 0 rect = Rect.malloc if GetWindowRect(child, rect) != 0 w = rect.right - rect.left h = rect.bottom - rect.top match = check_match(w, h, target_w, target_h, rect) return match if match end found_in_child = find_recursive(child, target_w, target_h) return found_in_child if found_in_child child = GetWindow(child, GW_HWNDNEXT) end nil end def self.check_match(w, h, target_w, target_h, rect) tol = 30 [1.0, 1.25, 1.5, 2.0].each do |scale| if (w - target_w * scale).abs <= tol && (h - target_h * scale).abs <= tol return { :x => rect.left, :y => rect.top, :w => w, :h => h, :scale => scale } end end nil end # Prepara o ambiente para gravação: esconde janelas sobrepostas e coloca SketchUp no topo def self.prepare_for_recording(rec_rect) return unless Sketchup.platform == :platform_win @hidden_windows = [] @topmost_hwnd = nil begin su_hwnd = GetForegroundWindow() return unless su_hwnd && su_hwnd.to_i != 0 # Obter PID do SketchUp pid_buf = Fiddle::Pointer.malloc(4) GetWindowThreadProcessId(su_hwnd, pid_buf) su_pid = pid_buf[0, 4].unpack('L')[0] # Colocar janela principal do SketchUp como TOPMOST SetWindowPos(su_hwnd, Fiddle::Pointer.new(-1), 0, 0, 0, 0, SWP_NOMOVE | SWP_NOSIZE) @topmost_hwnd = su_hwnd # Enumerar todas as janelas top-level e esconder as do mesmo processo que sobrepõem a área de gravação desktop = GetDesktopWindow() child = GetWindow(desktop, GW_CHILD) while child && child.to_i != 0 if child.to_i != su_hwnd.to_i && IsWindowVisible(child) != 0 pid_buf2 = Fiddle::Pointer.malloc(4) GetWindowThreadProcessId(child, pid_buf2) child_pid = pid_buf2[0, 4].unpack('L')[0] if child_pid == su_pid win_rect = Rect.malloc if GetWindowRect(child, win_rect) != 0 if rects_overlap?(win_rect, rec_rect) ShowWindow(child, SW_HIDE) @hidden_windows << child end end end end child = GetWindow(child, GW_HWNDNEXT) end rescue => e puts "WindowUtils.prepare_for_recording: #{e.message}" end end # Restaura janelas escondidas e remove TOPMOST def self.restore_after_recording return unless Sketchup.platform == :platform_win begin @hidden_windows.each { |hwnd| ShowWindow(hwnd, SW_SHOW) } @hidden_windows = [] if @topmost_hwnd SetWindowPos(@topmost_hwnd, Fiddle::Pointer.new(-2), 0, 0, 0, 0, SWP_NOMOVE | SWP_NOSIZE) @topmost_hwnd = nil end rescue => e puts "WindowUtils.restore_after_recording: #{e.message}" end end def self.rects_overlap?(win_rect, rec_rect) !(win_rect.right <= rec_rect[:x] || win_rect.left >= rec_rect[:x] + rec_rect[:w] || win_rect.bottom <= rec_rect[:y] || win_rect.top >= rec_rect[:y] + rec_rect[:h]) end end # --- Módulo ScreenCapture (GDI32) --- # Captures the SketchUp viewport via Win32 GDI and produces a BMP thumbnail # encoded as Base64, suitable for embedding in an HTML tag. # # Uses Fiddle::Importer (Ruby stdlib) to call gdi32.dll and user32.dll functions. # Also declares PrintWindow from user32.dll as a fallback for occluded windows. # # Fiddle type mapping reference (from fiddle/types.rb Win32Types): # HANDLE / HDC / HWND / HBITMAP -> void* (pointer-sized, correct for 32/64-bit) # DWORD -> unsigned long # BOOL -> int # UINT -> unsigned int # # The extern signatures below use 'void*' directly for all handle types # (HDC, HBITMAP, HGDIOBJ) so we do not need to include Fiddle::Win32Types # or add custom typealiases beyond what WindowUtils already defines. module ScreenCapture extend Fiddle::Importer if Sketchup.platform == :platform_win dlload 'gdi32.dll', 'user32.dll' # --- user32.dll functions --- # HDC GetDC(HWND hWnd) # hWnd=NULL(0) returns DC for entire screen extern 'void* GetDC(void*)' # int ReleaseDC(HWND hWnd, HDC hDC) extern 'int ReleaseDC(void*, void*)' # BOOL PrintWindow(HWND hwnd, HDC hdcBlt, UINT nFlags) # Asks the window to paint itself into the given DC. # Works even when the window is partially occluded (unlike BitBlt from screen DC). extern 'int PrintWindow(void*, void*, unsigned int)' # --- gdi32.dll functions --- # HDC CreateCompatibleDC(HDC hdc) extern 'void* CreateCompatibleDC(void*)' # HBITMAP CreateCompatibleBitmap(HDC hdc, int cx, int cy) extern 'void* CreateCompatibleBitmap(void*, int, int)' # HGDIOBJ SelectObject(HDC hdc, HGDIOBJ h) extern 'void* SelectObject(void*, void*)' # BOOL BitBlt(HDC hdc, int x, int y, int cx, int cy, # HDC hdcSrc, int x1, int y1, DWORD rop) extern 'int BitBlt(void*, int, int, int, int, void*, int, int, unsigned long)' # BOOL StretchBlt(HDC hdcDest, int xDest, int yDest, int wDest, int hDest, # HDC hdcSrc, int xSrc, int ySrc, int wSrc, int hSrc, DWORD rop) extern 'int StretchBlt(void*, int, int, int, int, void*, int, int, int, int, unsigned long)' # description.pt:
Este script analisa e reduz em massa a resolução de texturas muito grandes no SketchUp, otimizando drasticamente o consumo de memória de vídeo (VRAM) e o desempenho geral do modelo. É a ferramenta ideal para arquitetos e modeladores que lidam com arquivos pesados e travamentos causados pela importação de materiais de altíssima resolução.

Principais Funcionalidades:
- Redução em Lote: Permite aplicar o redimensionamento a múltiplas texturas simultaneamente, limitando-as a uma resolução máxima definida pelo usuário.
- Monitoramento Ativo (Observador): Identifica automaticamente a adição de novos materiais pesados ao modelo e exibe uma notificação sugerindo a redução imediata.
- Painel Analítico Completo: Interface interativa que lista todas as texturas, calcula o peso individual na memória (VRAM) e estima a economia possível após a redução.
- Redimensionamento Inteligente: Altera o tamanho das imagens mantendo a proporção original (aspect ratio) e preservando o mapeamento visual no modelo 3D.

Acesso no SketchUp: Menu Extensões > Gerenciador de Texturas.
description.en:
This script analyzes and bulk reduces the resolution of very large textures in SketchUp, dramatically optimizing video memory (VRAM) consumption and overall model performance. It's the ideal tool for architects and modelers dealing with heavy files and crashes caused by importing very high-resolution materials.

Key Features:
- Batch Reduction: Allows resizing to be applied to multiple textures simultaneously, limiting them to a maximum resolution defined by the user.
- Active Monitoring (Observer): Automatically identifies the addition of new heavy materials to the model and displays a notification suggesting immediate reduction.
- Complete Analytics Panel: Interactive interface that lists all textures, calculates the individual weight in memory (VRAM), and estimates the possible savings after reduction.
- Intelligent Resizing: Changes the size of images while maintaining the original aspect ratio and preserving the visual mapping in the 3D model.

Access in SketchUp: Extensions Menu > Texture Manager.
description.es:
Este script analiza y reduce la resolución de texturas muy grandes en SketchUp, optimizando drásticamente el consumo de memoria de vídeo (VRAM) y el rendimiento general del modelo. Es la herramienta ideal para arquitectos y modeladores que trabajan con archivos pesados ​​y sufren fallos al importar materiales de muy alta resolución.

Características principales:
- Reducción por lotes: Permite redimensionar varias texturas simultáneamente, limitándolas a una resolución máxima definida por el usuario.

- Monitorización activa (Observador): Identifica automáticamente la adición de nuevos materiales pesados ​​al modelo y muestra una notificación sugiriendo su reducción inmediata.

- Panel de análisis completo: Interfaz interactiva que muestra todas las texturas, calcula su peso individual en memoria (VRAM) y estima el ahorro potencial tras la reducción.

- Redimensionamiento inteligente: Cambia el tamaño de las imágenes manteniendo la relación de aspecto original y conservando el mapeo visual en el modelo 3D.

Acceso en SketchUp: Menú Extensiones > Gestor de texturas.

    def self.obter_texto(chave)
      locale = Sketchup.get_locale
      lang   = IDIOMAS.key?(locale) ? locale : 'en-US'
      IDIOMAS[lang][chave] || IDIOMAS['en-US'][chave] || chave.to_s
    end

    PLUGIN_VERSION = "1.0.0".freeze
    SETTINGS_KEY   = "ScriptUp_TextureManager".freeze

    @dialog          = nil
    @toast           = nil
    @observer        = nil
    @observer_active = false
    @max_resolution  = 2048
    @filter_min      = 1024
    @target_res      = 1024
    @toast_queue     = []
    @toast_busy      = false
    @processing      = false
    @notified_mats   = {}          # mat_name => Time.now — debounce por material
    @current_toast_mat = nil       # mat_name do toast atualmente exibido

    # ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
    # Persistência de configurações do usuário
    # ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

    def self.load_settings
      @filter_min      = Sketchup.read_default(SETTINGS_KEY, "filter_min", 1024).to_i
      @max_resolution  = Sketchup.read_default(SETTINGS_KEY, "max_resolution", 2048).to_i
      @target_res      = Sketchup.read_default(SETTINGS_KEY, "target_res", 1024).to_i
      @observer_active = Sketchup.read_default(SETTINGS_KEY, "observer_active", false) ? true : false
    end

    def self.save_setting(key, value)
      Sketchup.write_default(SETTINGS_KEY, key, value)
    end

    # ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
    # PNG Writer — cria PNG em Ruby puro (sem dependências externas)
    # ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

    def self.write_png_chunk(io, type, data)
      io.write([data.bytesize].pack('N'))
      chunk = type.dup.force_encoding('ASCII-8BIT') + data
      io.write(type)
      io.write(data)
      io.write([Zlib.crc32(chunk)].pack('N'))
    end

    def self.save_png(filepath, width, height, rows)
      raw = String.new(encoding: 'ASCII-8BIT')
      rows.each do |row|
        raw << "\x00".b # filtro: None
        raw << row
      end
      compressed = Zlib::Deflate.deflate(raw, Zlib::BEST_SPEED)
      File.open(filepath, 'wb') do |f|
        f.write([0x89, 0x50, 0x4E, 0x47, 0x0D, 0x0A, 0x1A, 0x0A].pack('C*'))
        ihdr = [width, height, 8, 6, 0, 0, 0].pack('NNCCCCC')
        write_png_chunk(f, 'IHDR', ihdr)
        write_png_chunk(f, 'IDAT', compressed)
        write_png_chunk(f, 'IEND', ''.b)
      end
    end

    # ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
    # Coleta de dados de texturas do modelo
    # ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

    def self.collect_texture_data
      model = Sketchup.active_model
      return [] unless model

      textures = []
      model.materials.each do |mat|
        next unless mat.texture
        t = mat.texture
        w = t.image_width
        h = t.image_height
        vram_bytes = w * h * 4 # RGBA na GPU
        textures << {
          name:       mat.display_name,
          mat_name:   mat.name,
          width:      w,
          height:     h,
          max_dim:    [w, h].max,
          pixels:     w * h,
          vram_bytes: vram_bytes,
          vram_mb:    (vram_bytes / (1024.0 * 1024)).round(2),
          color:      mat.color ? format('#%02X%02X%02X', mat.color.red, mat.color.green, mat.color.blue) : '#888888'
        }
      end
      textures.sort_by { |t| -t[:pixels] }
    end

    # ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
    # Redimensionamento de textura individual
    # ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

    def self.resize_material(material, max_dim)
      return [false, nil] unless material && material.texture

      texture = material.texture
      src_w = texture.image_width
      src_h = texture.image_height
      return [false, nil] if [src_w, src_h].max <= max_dim

      # Calcular novas dimensões mantendo proporção
      if src_w >= src_h
        new_w = max_dim
        new_h = [(src_h.to_f / src_w * max_dim).round, 1].max
      else
        new_h = max_dim
        new_w = [(src_w.to_f / src_h * max_dim).round, 1].max
      end

      image_rep = texture.image_rep

      # Usar dimensões reais do ImageRep para acessar dados binários corretamente
      # (podem diferir de texture.image_width/height em certos formatos)
      if image_rep.respond_to?(:width) && image_rep.respond_to?(:height)
        rep_w = image_rep.width
        rep_h = image_rep.height
        if rep_w > 0 && rep_h > 0 && (rep_w != src_w || rep_h != src_h)
          src_w = rep_w
          src_h = rep_h
          # Recalcular dimensões de saída com as dimensões reais
          if src_w >= src_h
            new_w = max_dim
            new_h = [(src_h.to_f / src_w * max_dim).round, 1].max
          else
            new_h = max_dim
            new_w = [(src_w.to_f / src_h * max_dim).round, 1].max
          end
        end
      end

      # Caminho rápido: acessar todos os pixels de uma vez via data (evita milhares de chamadas à API)
      if image_rep.respond_to?(:data)
        rows, swap_rb = resize_bulk(image_rep, src_w, src_h, new_w, new_h)
      elsif image_rep.respond_to?(:color_at_uv)
        rows = resize_uv(image_rep, new_w, new_h)
        swap_rb = false
      else
        puts "#{obter_texto(:plugin_name)}: #{obter_texto(:err_api)}"
        return [false, nil]
      end

      return [false, nil] unless rows

      # Escolher formato: JPG para texturas opacas (menor), PNG para texturas com alpha
      has_alpha = (image_rep.bits_per_pixel >= 32)
      ext = has_alpha ? "png" : "jpg"
      temp_path = File.join(Sketchup.temp_dir, "fptm_#{material.entityID}_#{Time.now.to_i}.#{ext}")
      temp_path = save_resized_image(temp_path, new_w, new_h, rows, swap_rb)

      # Preservar tamanho no modelo
      tw = texture.width
      th = texture.height

      material.texture = temp_path

      unless material.texture
        puts "#{obter_texto(:plugin_name)}: #{obter_texto(:err_atribuir)} '#{material.display_name}'"
        File.delete(temp_path) rescue nil
        return [false, nil]
      end

      material.texture.size = [tw, th]

      [true, temp_path]
    rescue => e
      puts "#{obter_texto(:plugin_name)}: #{obter_texto(:err_resize)} '#{material.display_name}': #{e.message}"
      puts e.backtrace.first(3).join("\n")
      [false, nil]
    end

    # Salvar imagem redimensionada: usa ImageRep#save_file (suporta JPG/PNG) com fallback para PNG puro
    # Rows estão em formato NATIVO do ImageRep (BGRA no Windows, RGBA no macOS), topo→baixo.
    # swap_rb indica se o formato nativo tem R/B invertidos em relação a RGBA (necessário apenas no fallback PNG).
    # Retorna o path real do arquivo salvo.
    def self.save_resized_image(filepath, new_w, new_h, rows, swap_rb)
      # Caminho principal: ImageRep#save_file (suporta JPG e PNG pela extensão)
      if defined?(Sketchup::ImageRep) && Sketchup::ImageRep.method_defined?(:save_file)
        begin
          # Rows já estão em formato nativo — apenas inverter ordem (topo→baixo → baixo→topo)
          native_data = rows.reverse.join

          # Validar tamanho do buffer antes de set_data
          expected_size = new_w * 4 * new_h
          if native_data.bytesize != expected_size
            if native_data.bytesize < expected_size
              native_data = native_data + ("\x00".b * (expected_size - native_data.bytesize))
            else
              native_data = native_data.byteslice(0, expected_size)
            end
          end

          rep = Sketchup::ImageRep.new
          rep.set_data(new_w, new_h, 32, 0, native_data)
          rep.save_file(filepath)
          return filepath
        rescue => e
          puts "#{obter_texto(:plugin_name)}: #{obter_texto(:err_save)}: #{e.message}"
        end
      end

      # Fallback: PNG precisa de RGBA — trocar R↔B se nativo é BGRA
      if swap_rb
        rgba_rows = rows.map do |row|
          bytes = row.unpack('C*')
          j = 0
          while j < bytes.length
            bytes[j], bytes[j + 2] = bytes[j + 2], bytes[j]
            j += 4
          end
          bytes.pack('C*')
        end
      else
        rgba_rows = rows
      end
      fallback_path = filepath.sub(/\.\w+$/, '.png')
      save_png(fallback_path, new_w, new_h, rgba_rows)
      fallback_path
    end

    # Reamostragem rápida: nearest-neighbor com byteslice (copia pixels inteiros)
    # Saída em formato NATIVO do ImageRep (sem conversão de canais)
    # Retorna [rows, swap_rb]
    def self.resize_bulk(image_rep, src_w, src_h, new_w, new_h)
      raw = image_rep.data
      bpp = image_rep.bits_per_pixel / 8
      bpp = 1 if bpp < 1
      row_padding = image_rep.row_padding
      src_stride = src_w * bpp + row_padding
      raw_size = raw.bytesize

      # Detectar se formato nativo tem R/B invertidos (BGRA no Windows)
      swap_rb = false
      if image_rep.respond_to?(:color_at_uv)
        ref = image_rep.color_at_uv(0.5, 0.5, false)
        if ref
          ref_off = (src_h / 2) * src_stride + (src_w / 2) * bpp
          byte0 = raw.getbyte(ref_off) || 0
          swap_rb = (byte0 == ref.blue && ref.blue != ref.red)
        end
      end

      # Pré-computar mapeamento de colunas (nearest-neighbor, constante para todas as linhas)
      x_off = Array.new(new_w)
      inv_w = src_w.to_f / new_w
      new_w.times do |x|
        sx = ((x + 0.5) * inv_w).floor
        sx = src_w - 1 if sx >= src_w
        x_off[x] = sx * bpp
      end

      # Pré-computar mapeamento de linhas (dados ImageRep são de baixo→topo; saída topo→baixo)
      y_base = Array.new(new_h)
      inv_h = 1.0 / new_h
      new_h.times do |y|
        v = 1.0 - ((y + 0.5) * inv_h)
        sy = (v * src_h).floor
        sy = src_h - 1 if sy >= src_h
        sy = 0 if sy < 0
        y_base[y] = sy * src_stride
      end

      # Nearest-neighbor: copiar pixels inteiros via byteslice (1 chamada por pixel, sem math)
      rows = Array.new(new_h)
      expected_row_size = new_w * 4
      zero_pixel = ("\x00" * 4).b.freeze
      
      if bpp >= 4
        # RGBA/BGRA: copiar 4 bytes por pixel diretamente
        new_h.times do |y|
          rb = y_base[y]
          row = String.new(capacity: expected_row_size, encoding: 'ASCII-8BIT')
          new_w.times do |x|
            off = rb + x_off[x]
            if off + 4 <= raw_size
              row << raw.byteslice(off, 4)
            else
              # Pixel fora dos limites do buffer — preencher com zeros
              chunk = off < raw_size ? raw.byteslice(off, raw_size - off) : nil
              row << (chunk ? chunk + ("\x00".b * (4 - chunk.bytesize)) : zero_pixel)
            end
          end
          rows[y] = row
        end
      else
        # RGB/BGR ou menos: copiar bpp bytes + completar para 4 bytes (RGBA)
        alpha_byte = "\xFF".b.freeze
        read_bytes = [bpp, 3].min
        new_h.times do |y|
          rb = y_base[y]
          row = String.new(capacity: expected_row_size, encoding: 'ASCII-8BIT')
          new_w.times do |x|
            off = rb + x_off[x]
            if off + read_bytes <= raw_size
              pixel = raw.byteslice(off, read_bytes)
            else
              pixel = off < raw_size ? raw.byteslice(off, raw_size - off) : "".b
            end
            # Completar para 3 bytes (RGB) se necessário, depois adicionar alpha
            pixel = pixel + ("\x00".b * (3 - pixel.bytesize)) if pixel.bytesize < 3
            row << pixel << alpha_byte
          end
          rows[y] = row
        end
      end

      [rows, swap_rb]
    rescue => e
      puts "#{obter_texto(:plugin_name)}: #{obter_texto(:err_bulk)}: #{e.message}"
      [nil, false]
    end

    # Fallback: reamostragem via color_at_uv (mais lento, para compatibilidade)
    def self.resize_uv(image_rep, new_w, new_h)
      rows = []
      new_h.times do |y|
        row = String.new(encoding: 'ASCII-8BIT')
        v = 1.0 - ((y + 0.5) / new_h.to_f)
        new_w.times do |x|
          u = (x + 0.5) / new_w.to_f
          c = image_rep.color_at_uv(u, v, true)
          if c
            row << [c.red, c.green, c.blue, c.alpha].pack('CCCC')
          else
            row << [0, 0, 0, 255].pack('CCCC')
          end
        end
        rows << row
      end
      rows
    end

    # ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
    # Redimensionamento em lote (com progresso via timer)
    # ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

    def self.resize_batch(mat_names, target_max)
      model = Sketchup.active_model
      return unless model

      materials = mat_names.map { |n| model.materials[n] }.compact.select { |m|
        m.texture && [m.texture.image_width, m.texture.image_height].max > target_max
      }
      return if materials.empty?

      # Pausar observador durante o processamento em lote
      @processing = true

      model.start_operation(obter_texto(:op_reduzir_batch), true)
      total = materials.length
      processed = 0
      successes = 0
      temp_files = []

      process_next = proc {
        if processed < total
          mat = materials[processed]
          ok, temp_path = resize_material(mat, target_max)
          successes += 1 if ok
          temp_files << temp_path if temp_path
          processed += 1
          if @dialog
            @dialog.execute_script("updateProgress(#{processed}, #{total})")
          end
          UI.start_timer(0.01, false) { process_next.call }
        else
          model.commit_operation
          # Forçar atualização visual do modelo
          model.active_view.invalidate if model.active_view
          # Limpar arquivos temporários somente após o commit
          temp_files.each { |f| File.delete(f) rescue nil }
          if @dialog
            @dialog.execute_script("resizeComplete(#{successes}, #{total})")
          end
          UI.start_timer(0.3, false) { send_texture_data if @dialog }
          # Retomar observador após delay maior que os timers do observer (0.5s)
          # para garantir que callbacks pendentes sejam ignorados
          UI.start_timer(1.5, false) { @processing = false }
        end
      }

      process_next.call
    end

    # ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
    # Comunicação Ruby → JS
    # ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

    def self.send_texture_data
      return unless @dialog
      data = collect_texture_data
      json = JSON.generate(data)
      @dialog.execute_script("receiveTextureData(#{json})")
    end

    def self.send_observer_state
      return unless @dialog
      @dialog.execute_script("receiveSettings(#{@observer_active}, #{@max_resolution}, #{@filter_min}, #{@target_res})")
    end

    # ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
    # Observador de materiais
    # ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

    class TextureObserver < Sketchup::MaterialsObserver
      def onMaterialAdd(materials, material)
        UI.start_timer(0.5, false) {
          GerenciadorDeTexturas.check_new_material(material)
        }
      end

      def onMaterialChange(materials, material)
        UI.start_timer(0.5, false) {
          GerenciadorDeTexturas.check_new_material(material)
        }
      end
    end

    class AppObserverHandler < Sketchup::AppObserver
      def onNewModel(model)
        GerenciadorDeTexturas.reinit_observer_for_model(model)
      end

      def onOpenModel(model)
        GerenciadorDeTexturas.reinit_observer_for_model(model)
      end
    end

    def self.check_new_material(material)
      return unless @observer_active
      return if @processing
      return unless material && material.valid? && material.texture

      t = material.texture
      max_dim = [t.image_width, t.image_height].max
      return if max_dim <= @max_resolution

      mat_name = material.name

      # Debounce: ignorar se já notificamos este material nos últimos 5 segundos
      if @notified_mats[mat_name] && (Time.now - @notified_mats[mat_name]) < 5
        return
      end

      # Ignorar se já está na fila ou sendo exibido no toast atual
      return if @toast_queue.any? { |info| info[:mat_name] == mat_name }
      return if @current_toast_mat == mat_name

      @notified_mats[mat_name] = Time.now
      show_toast(material)
    end

    def self.toggle_observer(active, max_res = nil)
      model = Sketchup.active_model
      return unless model

      @max_resolution = max_res.to_i if max_res && max_res.to_i > 0

      if active && !@observer_active
        @observer = TextureObserver.new
        model.materials.add_observer(@observer)
        @observer_active = true
      elsif !active && @observer_active
        model.materials.remove_observer(@observer) if @observer
        @observer = nil
        @observer_active = false
      end

      save_setting("observer_active", @observer_active)
      save_setting("max_resolution", @max_resolution)
      send_observer_state if @dialog
    end

    def self.reinit_observer_for_model(model)
      return unless model
      return unless @observer_active

      # Remove observador antigo (se existir) — ele estava no modelo anterior
      @observer = nil
      @observer_active = false

      # Reanexa no novo modelo
      toggle_observer(true, @max_resolution)

      # Atualiza dados da dialog se estiver aberta
      if @dialog
        UI.start_timer(0.3, false) {
          send_texture_data
          send_observer_state
        }
      end
    end

    # ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
    # Toast notification (mini HtmlDialog)
    # ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

    def self.show_toast(material)
      t = material.texture
      info = {
        name:     material.display_name,
        mat_name: material.name,
        width:    t.image_width,
        height:   t.image_height,
        vram_mb:  (t.image_width * t.image_height * 4.0 / (1024 * 1024)).round(1),
        max_res:  @max_resolution,
        color:    material.color ? format('#%02X%02X%02X', material.color.red, material.color.green, material.color.blue) : '#888'
      }

      @toast_queue << info
      process_toast_queue unless @toast_busy
    end

    def self.process_toast_queue
      return if @toast_queue.empty?
      @toast_busy = true
      info = @toast_queue.shift
      @current_toast_mat = info[:mat_name]

      @toast.close if @toast && @toast.visible?
      @toast = UI::HtmlDialog.new(
        dialog_title: obter_texto(:toast_title),
        width:        400,
        height:       175,
        resizable:    false,
        style:        UI::HtmlDialog::STYLE_UTILITY
      )

      @toast.set_html(toast_html(info))

      @toast.add_action_callback("reduce") do |_ctx|
        mat = Sketchup.active_model.materials[info[:mat_name]]
        if mat
          @processing = true
          Sketchup.active_model.start_operation(obter_texto(:op_reduzir_single), true)
          ok, temp_path = resize_material(mat, @max_resolution)
          Sketchup.active_model.commit_operation
          Sketchup.active_model.active_view.invalidate
          File.delete(temp_path) rescue nil if temp_path
          # Delay para ignorar callbacks pendentes do observer
          UI.start_timer(1.5, false) { @processing = false }
          send_texture_data if @dialog
        end
        @toast.close rescue nil
        @current_toast_mat = nil
        @toast_busy = false
        UI.start_timer(0.3, false) { process_toast_queue }
      end

      @toast.add_action_callback("ignore") do |_ctx|
        @toast.close rescue nil
        @current_toast_mat = nil
        @toast_busy = false
        UI.start_timer(0.3, false) { process_toast_queue }
      end

      @toast.add_action_callback("auto_dismiss") do |_ctx|
        @current_toast_mat = nil
        @toast_busy = false
        UI.start_timer(0.3, false) { process_toast_queue }
      end

      @toast.show
    end

    # ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
    # HTML — Toast
    # ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

    def self.toast_html(info)
      <<~HTML
      <!DOCTYPE html>
      <html>
      <head>
      <meta charset="UTF-8">
      <style>
        * { margin: 0; padding: 0; box-sizing: border-box; }

        }

          from { transform: translateX(100px); opacity: 0; }

        }
        .header {
          display: flex;
          align-items: center;
          gap: 10px;
          margin-bottom: 10px;
        }
        .icon { font-size: 16px; }
        .title { font-size: 14px; font-weight: bold; color: #00567e; }
        .info {
          display: flex;
          align-items: center;
          gap: 10px;
          margin-bottom: 10px;
        }
        .swatch {
          width: 32px;
          height: 32px;
          border-radius: 4px;
          border: 1px solid #cccccc;
          flex-shrink: 0;
        }
        .details { flex: 1; min-width: 0; }
        .mat-name {
          font-size: 14px;

        }
        .mat-res {
          font-size: 12px;
          color: #666666;
          margin-top: 2px;
        }
        .mat-res strong { color: #00567e; font-weight: bold; }
        .actions {
          display: flex;
          gap: 10px;
          align-items: center;
        }
        button {
          border: none;
          border-radius: 4px;
          padding: 10px 20px;
          font-size: 14px;
          cursor: pointer;
          transition: all 0.15s;
        }
        .btn-reduce {
          background: #f3b359;
          color: white;
          flex: 1;
        }
        .btn-reduce:hover { background: #e3a752; }
        .btn-ignore {
          background: #9e9e9e;
          color: white;
          flex: 1;
        }
        .btn-ignore:hover { background: #7d7d7d; }
        .timer-bar {
          position: absolute;
          bottom: 0;
          left: 4px;
          right: 0;
          height: 3px;
          background: rgba(243, 179, 89, 0.2);
          border-radius: 0 0 4px 4px;
          overflow: hidden;
        }
        .timer-fill {
          height: 100%;
          background: #f3b359;
          border-radius: 0 0 4px 4px;
          animation: countdown 10s linear forwards;
        }
        @keyframes countdown {
          from { width: 100%; }
          to   { width: 0%; }
        }
      </style>
      </head>
      <body>
        <div class="toast">
          <div class="header">
            <span class="icon">⚠️</span>
            <span class="title">#{obter_texto(:toast_header)}</span>
          </div>

            <div class="swatch" style="background: #{info[:color]};"></div>
            <div class="details">
              <div class="mat-name">#{info[:name]}</div>
              <div class="mat-res"><strong>#{info[:width]} × #{info[:height]}</strong>  · 
  #{info[:vram_mb]} MB  ·  #{obter_texto(:toast_limit)}: #{info[:max_res]}px</div>
            </div>
          </div>
          <div class="actions">
            <button class="btn-reduce" onclick="sketchup.reduce()">#{obter_texto(:toast_btn_reduce)} #{info[:max_res]}px</button>
            <button class="btn-ignore" onclick="sketchup.ignore()">#{obter_texto(:toast_btn_ignore)}</button>
          </div>

        </div>
        <script>
          setTimeout(function() { sketchup.auto_dismiss(); }, 10000);
        </script>
      </body>
      </html>
      HTML
    end

    def self.main_html

                <input type="number" id="filterMin" value="1024" min="0" step="1" onchange="applyFilter()" onkeydown="if(event.key==='Enter'){applyFilter();this.blur();}" onfocus="this.select();openDropdown(this)" data-options="0:#{obter_texto(:ctrl_all)},256,512,1024,2048,4096">
                <span class="dropdown-arrow" onmousedown="toggleDropdown(event, this)">▼</span>
                <div class="dropdown-panel"></div>
              </div>
              <span class="unit-label">px</span>
            </div>

            <div class="control-group">
              <label>#{obter_texto(:ctrl_limit)}</label>
              <div class="input-with-dropdown">
                <input type="number" id="maxResLimit" value="2048" min="1" step="1" onchange="updateLimit()" onkeydown="if(event.key==='Enter'){updateLimit();this.blur();}" onfocus="this.select();openDropdown(this)" data-options="256,512,1024,2048,4096">
                <span class="dropdown-arrow" onmousedown="toggleDropdown(event, this)">▼</span>
                <div class="dropdown-panel"></div>
              </div>
              <span class="unit-label">px</span>
            </div>

            <span class="spacer"></span>

            <div class="obs-toggle" id="obsToggle" onclick="toggleObserver()">
              <div class="obs-dot"></div>
              <span class="obs-label">#{obter_texto(:ctrl_obs)}</span>
            </div>
          </div>

          <div class="list-container" id="listContainer">
            <div class="tex-header">
              <span></span>
              <span>#{obter_texto(:col_mat)}</span>
              <span>#{obter_texto(:col_res)}</span>
              <span style="text-align:right">VRAM</span>
              <span style="text-align:right">#{obter_texto(:col_weight)}</span>
            </div>
            <div id="texList"></div>
          </div>

          <div class="action-bar">
            <span class="info-text">
              <span id="selectedInfo">0 #{obter_texto(:js_sel_plur)}</span>
               · 
              #{obter_texto(:lbl_saving)} <strong id="savingInfo">0 MB</strong>
            </span>
            <span class="spacer"></span>
            <div class="control-group">
              <label>#{obter_texto(:lbl_reduce_to)}</label>
              <div class="input-with-dropdown">
                <input type="number" id="targetRes" value="1024" min="1" step="1" onchange="updateTargetRes()" onkeydown="if(event.key==='Enter'){updateTargetRes();this.blur();}" onfocus="this.select();openDropdown(this)" data-options="256,512,1024,2048,4096">
                <span class="dropdown-arrow" onmousedown="toggleDropdown(event, this)">▼</span>
                <div class="dropdown-panel"></div>
              </div>
              <span class="unit-label">px</span>
            </div>
            <button class="btn btn-secondary" onclick="selectAll()">#{obter_texto(:btn_sel_filtered)}</button>
            <button class="btn btn-primary" id="btnReduce" onclick="reduceSelected()" disabled>
              #{obter_texto(:btn_reduce_sel)}
            </button>
          </div>

          <div class="progress-overlay" id="progressOverlay">
            <div class="progress-card">
              <div class="progress-title">#{obter_texto(:prog_title)}</div>
              <div class="progress-sub" id="progressSub">#{obter_texto(:prog_sub)}</div>
              <div class="progress-bar-bg">
                <div class="progress-bar-fill" id="progressFill"></div>
              </div>
              <div class="progress-count" id="progressCount"></div>
            </div>
          </div>
        </div>

        <script>
          let allTextures = [];
          let selected = new Set();
          let filterMin = 1024;
          // ── Receive data from Ruby ──
          function receiveTextureData(data) {
            allTextures = data;
            selected.clear();
            renderList();
            updateStats();
            updateActions();
          }

          function receiveSettings(obsActive, maxRes, filterVal, targetVal) {
            // Aplicar configurações salvas
            document.getElementById('filterMin').value = filterVal;
            document.getElementById('maxResLimit').value = maxRes;
            document.getElementById('targetRes').value = targetVal;
            filterMin = filterVal;

            const el = document.getElementById('obsToggle');
            if (obsActive) el.classList.add('active');
            else el.classList.remove('active');

            renderList();
            updateStats();
            updateActions();
          }

          // ── Filtering ──
          function applyFilter() {
            filterMin = parseInt(document.getElementById('filterMin').value) || 0;
            selected.clear();
            renderList();
            updateActions();
            sketchup.save_setting('filter_min', filterMin);
          }

          function getFiltered() {
            return allTextures.filter(t => t.max_dim > filterMin);
          }

          // ── Render ──
          function renderList() {
            const list = document.getElementById('texList');
            const filtered = getFiltered();
            const limit = parseInt(document.getElementById('maxResLimit').value) || 2048;
            const totalVram = allTextures.reduce((s, t) => s + t.vram_bytes, 0) || 1;
            if (filtered.length === 0) {
              list.innerHTML = '<div class="empty-state"><div class="icon">📭</div><p>#{obter_texto(:js_empty)}</p></div>';
              return;
            }

            list.innerHTML = filtered.map(t => {
              const isSelected = selected.has(t.mat_name);
              const cls = t.max_dim > limit ? 'big' : (t.max_dim > limit / 2 ? 'med' : 'ok');
              const pct = ((t.vram_bytes / totalVram) * 100);
              const barColor = cls === 'big' ? '#ff4d4d' : (cls === 'med' ? '#f3b359' : '#2d7a2d');

              return '<div class="tex-row' + (isSelected ? ' selected' : '') + '" onclick="toggleSelect(\\''+t.mat_name.replace(/'/g, "\\\\'")+'\\')">'+
                '<div class="tex-check">' + (isSelected ? '✓' : '') + '</div>' +
                '<div class="tex-name">' +
                  '<div class="tex-swatch" style="background:'+t.color+'"></div>' +
                  '<span class="tex-display-name" title="'+t.name+'">'+t.name+'</span>' +
                '</div>' +
                '<div class="tex-res '+cls+'">'+t.width+' × '+t.height+'</div>' +
                '<div class="tex-vram">'+t.vram_mb+' MB</div>' +
                '<div class="tex-pct">' +
                  '<div style="font-size:12px;color:#999999;text-align:right">'+pct.toFixed(1)+'%</div>' +
                  '<div class="tex-pct-bar"><div class="tex-pct-fill" style="width:'+pct+'%;background:'+barColor+'"></div></div>' +
                '</div>' +
              '</div>';
            }).join('');
          }

          // ── Selection ──
          function toggleSelect(matName) {
            if (selected.has(matName)) selected.delete(matName);
            else selected.add(matName);
            renderList();
            updateActions();
          }

          function selectAll() {
            const filtered = getFiltered();
            const limit = parseInt(document.getElementById('maxResLimit').value) || 2048;
            const overLimit = filtered.filter(t => t.max_dim > limit);
            if (overLimit.length > 0 && overLimit.every(t => selected.has(t.mat_name))) {
              overLimit.forEach(t => selected.delete(t.mat_name));
            } else {
              overLimit.forEach(t => selected.add(t.mat_name));
            }
            renderList();
            updateActions();
          }

          // ── Stats ──
          function updateStats() {
            const limit = parseInt(document.getElementById('maxResLimit').value) || 2048;
            const totalVram = allTextures.reduce((s, t) => s + t.vram_bytes, 0);
            const overLimit = allTextures.filter(t => t.max_dim > limit);
            const potentialSaving = overLimit.reduce((s, t) => {
              const factor = Math.pow(limit / t.max_dim, 2);
              return s + t.vram_bytes * (1 - factor);
            }, 0);
            document.getElementById('statCount').textContent = allTextures.length;
            document.getElementById('statVram').textContent = formatMB(totalVram);
            document.getElementById('statOver').textContent = overLimit.length;
            document.getElementById('statSaving').textContent = formatMB(potentialSaving);
          }

          function updateActions() {
            const targetMax = parseInt(document.getElementById('targetRes').value) || 1024;
            const selArr = allTextures.filter(t => selected.has(t.mat_name) && t.max_dim > targetMax);
            const saving = selArr.reduce((s, t) => {
              const factor = Math.pow(targetMax / t.max_dim, 2);
              return s + t.vram_bytes * (1 - factor);
            }, 0);
            document.getElementById('selectedInfo').textContent = selected.size + (selected.size !== 1 ? '#{obter_texto(:js_sel_plur)}' : '#{obter_texto(:js_sel_sing)}');
            document.getElementById('savingInfo').textContent = formatMB(saving);
            document.getElementById('btnReduce').disabled = selArr.length === 0;
          }

          function updateLimit() {
            const maxRes = parseInt(document.getElementById('maxResLimit').value) || 2048;
            // Sync target resolution with limit value
            document.getElementById('targetRes').value = maxRes;
            updateTargetRes();
            updateStats();
            renderList();
            updateActions();
            sketchup.update_limit(maxRes);
            sketchup.save_setting('max_resolution', maxRes);
          }

          function updateTargetRes() {
            updateActions();
            const target = parseInt(document.getElementById('targetRes').value) || 1024;
            sketchup.save_setting('target_res', target);
          }

          function formatMB(bytes) {
            const mb = bytes / (1024 * 1024);
            if (mb >= 1024) return (mb / 1024).toFixed(1) + ' GB';
            if (mb >= 1) return mb.toFixed(1) + ' MB';
            return (mb * 1024).toFixed(0) + ' KB';
          }

          // ── Actions ──
          function reduceSelected() {
            const targetMax = parseInt(document.getElementById('targetRes').value) || 1024;
            const names = Array.from(selected);
            if (names.length === 0) return;

            document.getElementById('progressOverlay').classList.add('show');
            document.getElementById('progressFill').style.width = '0%';
            document.getElementById('progressSub').textContent = '#{obter_texto(:js_prep)}';
            document.getElementById('progressCount').textContent = '0 / ' + names.length;

            sketchup.reduce_batch(JSON.stringify(names), targetMax);
          }

          function updateProgress(current, total) {
            const pct = (current / total * 100).toFixed(0);
            document.getElementById('progressFill').style.width = pct + '%';
            document.getElementById('progressSub').textContent = '#{obter_texto(:js_proc)} ' + current + ' #{obter_texto(:js_of)} ' + total;
            document.getElementById('progressCount').textContent = current + ' / ' + total;
          }

          function resizeComplete(successes, total) {
            document.getElementById('progressOverlay').classList.remove('show');
            selected.clear();

            const banner = document.getElementById('completeBanner');
            document.getElementById('completeText').textContent =
              successes + ' #{obter_texto(:js_of)} ' + total + ' ' + (total > 1 ? '#{obter_texto(:js_tex_plur)}' : '#{obter_texto(:js_tex_sing)}') + ' ' + (total > 1 ? '#{obter_texto(:js_red_plur)}' : '#{obter_texto(:js_red_sing)}') + ' #{obter_texto(:js_success)}';
            banner.classList.add('show');

            setTimeout(function() { banner.classList.remove('show'); }, 6000);
          }

          function hideBanner() {
            document.getElementById('completeBanner').classList.remove('show');
          }

          function toggleObserver() {
            var el = document.getElementById('obsToggle');
            var active = !el.classList.contains('active');
            var maxRes = parseInt(document.getElementById('maxResLimit').value) || 2048;
            sketchup.toggle_observer(active, maxRes);
          }

          // ── Custom dropdown helpers ──
          function buildDropdownOptions(input) {
            var panel = input.parentElement.querySelector('.dropdown-panel');
            panel.innerHTML = '';
            var opts = (input.getAttribute('data-options') || '').split(',');
            opts.forEach(function(opt) {
              var parts = opt.split(':');
              var val = parts[0].trim();
              var label = parts.length > 1 ? val + ' (' + parts[1].trim() + ')' : val;
              var div = document.createElement('div');
              div.className = 'dropdown-option';
              div.textContent = label;
              div.setAttribute('data-value', val);
              div.onmousedown = function(e) {
                e.preventDefault();
                input.value = val;
                closeAllDropdowns();
                input.dispatchEvent(new Event('change'));
                input.blur();
              };
              panel.appendChild(div);
            });
          }

          function openDropdown(input) {
            closeAllDropdowns();
            buildDropdownOptions(input);
            var panel = input.parentElement.querySelector('.dropdown-panel');
            panel.classList.remove('drop-up');
            panel.classList.add('open');
            // If dropdown goes beyond viewport, open upward
            var rect = panel.getBoundingClientRect();
            if (rect.bottom > window.innerHeight) {
              panel.classList.add('drop-up');
            }
          }

          function toggleDropdown(e, arrow) {
            e.preventDefault();
            var input = arrow.parentElement.querySelector('input');
            var panel = arrow.parentElement.querySelector('.dropdown-panel');
            if (panel.classList.contains('open')) {
              panel.classList.remove('open');
            } else {
              input.focus();
            }
          }

          function closeAllDropdowns() {
            var panels = document.querySelectorAll('.dropdown-panel');
            for (var i = 0; i < panels.length; i++) {
              panels[i].classList.remove('open');
            }
          }

          document.addEventListener('click', function(e) {
            if (!e.target.closest('.input-with-dropdown')) {
              closeAllDropdowns();
            }
          });

          // Close dropdown on blur (with delay to allow option click)
          document.querySelectorAll('.input-with-dropdown input').forEach(function(input) {
            input.addEventListener('blur', function() {
              setTimeout(closeAllDropdowns, 150);
            });
          });

          // ── Init ──
          sketchup.ready();
        </script>
      </body>
      </html>
      HTML
    end

    # ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
    # Diálogo principal
    # ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

    def self.show_dialog
      if @dialog && @dialog.visible?
        @dialog.bring_to_front
        return
      end

      @dialog = UI::HtmlDialog.new(
        dialog_title: obter_texto(:plugin_name),
        width:        680,
        height:       620,
        min_width:    500,
        min_height:   400,
        resizable:    true,
        style:        UI::HtmlDialog::STYLE_DIALOG
      )

      @dialog.set_html(main_html)

      # ── Callbacks ──

      @dialog.add_action_callback("ready") do |_ctx|
        load_settings
        send_texture_data
        send_observer_state
        # Auto-iniciar observador se estava ativo na última sessão
        if @observer_active && !@observer
          @observer_active = false # reset para toggle_observer ativar
          toggle_observer(true, @max_resolution)
        end
      end

      @dialog.add_action_callback("reduce_batch") do |_ctx, names_json, target_max|
        names = JSON.parse(names_json)
        resize_batch(names, target_max.to_i)
      end

      @dialog.add_action_callback("toggle_observer") do |_ctx, active, max_res|
        toggle_observer(active, max_res.to_i)
      end

      @dialog.add_action_callback("update_limit") do |_ctx, max_res|
        @max_resolution = max_res.to_i
        save_setting("max_resolution", @max_resolution)
      end

      @dialog.add_action_callback("save_setting") do |_ctx, key, value|
        val = value.to_s
        # Converter para inteiro se for numérico
        val = val.to_i if val =~ /\A\d+\z/
        save_setting(key, val)
        # Atualizar variável interna correspondente
        case key
        when "filter_min"
          @filter_min = val.to_i
        when "max_resolution"
          @max_resolution = val.to_i
        when "target_res"
          @target_res = val.to_i
        end
      end

      @dialog.set_on_closed {
        @dialog = nil
      }

      @dialog.show
    end

    # ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
    # Registro no menu
    # ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

    def self.executar
      unless @menu_loaded
        plugins_menu = UI.menu("Plugins")
        plugins_menu.add_item(obter_texto(:plugin_name)) { self.show_dialog }
        Sketchup.add_observer(AppObserverHandler.new)
        load_settings
        # Auto-iniciar observador se estava ativo na última sessão
        if @observer_active
          @observer_active = false
          toggle_observer(true, @max_resolution)
        end
        @menu_loaded = true
      end
    end

  end # module GerenciadorDeTexturas
end # module ScriptUpComunidade

ScriptUpComunidade::GerenciadorDeTexturas.executar
